structural_claim: Continuous systems spontaneously organize into discrete, hierarchical structures through symmetry-breaking instabilities driven by competing forces

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Dark matter halos form hierarchically through gravitational instability of density peaks, creating discrete structures from continuous fields
Gravitational instability naturally creates a web topology with matter flowing along filaments into nodes
Rotating flows undergo hierarchy of symmetry-breaking instabilities creating ordered structures
Thermal convection self-organizes into cellular patterns when buoyancy overcomes viscous damping
